verses.latin_vulgate: Nolite plures magistri fieri fratres mei, scientes quoniam majus judicium sumitis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μὴ πολλοὶ διδάσκαλοι γίνεσθε ἀδελφοί μου εἰδότες ὅτι μεῖζον κρίμα ληψόμεθα
verses.king_james: My brethren, be not many masters, knowing that we shall receive the greater condemnation.

verses.latin_vulgate: In multis enim offendimus omnes. Si quis in verbo non offendit, hic perfectus est vir: potest etiam freno circumducere totum corpus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πολλὰ γὰρ πταίομεν ἅπαντες εἴ τις ἐν λόγῳ οὐ πταίει οὗτος τέλειος ἀνήρ δυνατὸς χαλιναγωγῆσαι καὶ ὅλον τὸ σῶμα
verses.king_james: For in many things we offend all. If any man offend not in word, the same *is* a perfect man, *and* able also to bridle the whole body.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si autem equis frena in ora mittimus ad consentiendum nobis, et omne corpus illorum circumferimus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἰδού τῶν ἵππων τοὺς χαλινοὺς εἰς τὰ στόματα βάλλομεν πρὸς τὸ πείθεσθαι αὐτοὺς ἡμῖν καὶ ὅλον τὸ σῶμα αὐτῶν μετάγομεν
verses.king_james: Behold, we put bits in the horses' mouths, that they may obey us; and we turn about their whole body.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ecce et naves, cum magnæ sint, et a ventis validis minentur, circumferuntur a modico gubernaculo ubi impetus dirigentis voluerit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἰδού καὶ τὰ πλοῖα τηλικαῦτα ὄντα καὶ ὑπὸ σκληρῶν ἀνέμων ἐλαυνόμενα μετάγεται ὑπὸ ἐλαχίστου πηδαλίου ὅπου ἂν ἡ ὁρμὴ τοῦ εὐθύνοντος βούληται
verses.king_james: Behold also the ships, which though *they be* so great, and *are* driven of fierce winds, yet are they turned about with a very small helm, whithersoever the governor listeth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ita et lingua modicum quidem membrum est, et magna exaltat. Ecce quantus ignis quam magnam silvam incendit!
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὕτως καὶ ἡ γλῶσσα μικρὸν μέλος ἐστὶν καὶ μεγάλαυχεῖ ἰδού ὀλίγον πῦρ ἡλίκην ὕλην ἀνάπτει
verses.king_james: Even so the tongue is a little member, and boasteth great things. Behold, how great a matter a little fire kindleth!

verses.latin_vulgate: Et lingua ignis est, universitas iniquitatis. Lingua constituitur in membris nostris, quæ maculat totum corpus, et inflammat rotam nativitatis nostræ inflammata a gehenna.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἡ γλῶσσα πῦρ ὁ κόσμος τῆς ἀδικίας οὕτως ἡ γλῶσσα καθίσταται ἐν τοῖς μέλεσιν ἡμῶν ἡ σπιλοῦσα ὅλον τὸ σῶμα καὶ φλογίζουσα τὸν τροχὸν τῆς γενέσεως καὶ φλογιζομένη ὑπὸ τῆς γεέννης
verses.king_james: And the tongue *is* a fire, a world of iniquity: so is the tongue among our members, that it defileth the whole body, and setteth on fire the course of nature; and it is set on fire of hell.

verses.latin_vulgate: Omnis enim natura bestiarum, et volucrum, et serpentium, et ceterorum domantur, et domita sunt a natura humana:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πᾶσα γὰρ φύσις θηρίων τε καὶ πετεινῶν ἑρπετῶν τε καὶ ἐναλίων δαμάζεται καὶ δεδάμασται τῇ φύσει τῇ ἀνθρωπίνῃ
verses.king_james: For every kind of beasts, and of birds, and of serpents, and of things in the sea, is tamed, and hath been tamed of mankind:

verses.latin_vulgate: linguam autem nullus hominum domare potest: inquietum malum, plena veneno mortifero.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τὴν δὲ γλῶσσαν οὐδεὶς δύναται ἀνθρώπων δαμάσαι ἀκατάσχετον κακόν μεστὴ ἰοῦ θανατηφόρου
verses.king_james: But the tongue can no man tame; *it is* an unruly evil, full of deadly poison.

verses.latin_vulgate: In ipsa benedicimus Deum et Patrem: et in ipsa maledicimus homines, qui ad similitudinem Dei facti sunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐν αὐτῇ εὐλογοῦμεν τὸν θεὸν καὶ πατέρα καὶ ἐν αὐτῇ καταρώμεθα τοὺς ἀνθρώπους τοὺς καθ᾽ ὁμοίωσιν θεοῦ γεγονότας
verses.king_james: Therewith bless we God, even the Father; and therewith curse we men, which are made after the similitude of God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ex ipso ore procedit benedictio et maledictio. Non oportet, fratres mei, hæc ita fieri.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐκ τοῦ αὐτοῦ στόματος ἐξέρχεται εὐλογία καὶ κατάρα οὐ χρή ἀδελφοί μου ταῦτα οὕτως γίνεσθαι
verses.king_james: Out of the same mouth proceedeth blessing and cursing. My brethren, these things ought not so to be.

verses.latin_vulgate: Numquid fons de eodem foramine emanat dulcem et amaram aquam?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μήτι ἡ πηγὴ ἐκ τῆς αὐτῆς ὀπῆς βρύει τὸ γλυκὺ καὶ τὸ πικρόν
verses.king_james: Doth a fountain send forth at the same place sweet *water* and bitter?

verses.latin_vulgate: Numquid potest, fratres mei, ficus uvas facere, aut vitis ficus? Sic neque salsa dulcem potest facere aquam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μὴ δύναται ἀδελφοί μου συκῆ ἐλαίας ποιῆσαι ἢ ἄμπελος σῦκα οὕτως οὐδεμια πηγὴ ἁλυκὸν καὶ γλυκὺ ποιῆσαι ὕδωρ
verses.king_james: Can the fig tree, my brethren, bear olive berries? either a vine, figs? so *can* no fountain both yield salt water and fresh.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quis sapiens et disciplinatus inter vos? Ostendat ex bona conversatione operationem suam in mansuetudine sapientiæ.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τίς σοφὸς καὶ ἐπιστήμων ἐν ὑμῖν δειξάτω ἐκ τῆς καλῆς ἀναστροφῆς τὰ ἔργα αὐτοῦ ἐν πραΰτητι σοφίας
verses.king_james: Who *is* a wise man and endued with knowledge among you? let him shew out of a good conversation his works with meekness of wisdom.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quod si zelum amarum habetis, et contentiones sint in cordibus vestris: nolite gloriari, et mendaces esse adversus veritatem:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ δὲ ζῆλον πικρὸν ἔχετε καὶ ἐριθείαν ἐν τῇ καρδίᾳ ὑμῶν μὴ κατακαυχᾶσθε καὶ ψεύδεσθε κατὰ τῆς ἀληθείας
verses.king_james: But if ye have bitter envying and strife in your hearts, glory not, and lie not against the truth.

verses.latin_vulgate: non est enim ista sapientia desursum descendens: sed terrena, animalis, diabolica.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐκ ἔστιν αὕτη ἡ σοφία ἄνωθεν κατερχομένη ἀλλ᾽ ἐπίγειος ψυχική δαιμονιώδης
verses.king_james: This wisdom descendeth not from above, but *is* earthly, sensual, devilish.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ubi enim zelus et contentio, ibi inconstantia et omne opus pravum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅπου γὰρ ζῆλος καὶ ἐριθεία ἐκεῖ ἀκαταστασία καὶ πᾶν φαῦλον πρᾶγμα
verses.king_james: For where envying and strife *is*, there *is* confusion and every evil work.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quæ autem desursum est sapientia, primum quidem pudica est, deinde pacifica, modesta, suadibilis, bonus consentiens, plena misericordia et fructibus bonis, non judicans, sine simulatione.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἡ δὲ ἄνωθεν σοφία πρῶτον μὲν ἁγνή ἐστιν ἔπειτα εἰρηνική ἐπιεικής εὐπειθής μεστὴ ἐλέους καὶ καρπῶν ἀγαθῶν ἀδιάκριτος καὶ ἀνυπόκριτος
verses.king_james: But the wisdom that is from above is first pure, then peaceable, gentle, *and* easy to be intreated, full of mercy and good fruits, without partiality, and without hypocrisy.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fructus autem justitiæ, in pace seminatur, facientibus pacem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καρπὸς δὲ τῆς δικαιοσύνης ἐν εἰρήνῃ σπείρεται τοῖς ποιοῦσιν εἰρήνην
verses.king_james: And the fruit of righteousness is sown in peace of them that make peace.
